channel.title: Georgetown on The Guyana Brief
channel.description: Recent content in Georgetown on The Guyana Brief

item.title: DJ Roadblock — Friday, April 10, 2026
item.description: Your Friday traffic situation with DJ Roadblock: East Bank lockdown, the Camp Street crawl, Sheriff Street surprises, and why the Kitty roundabout is still an unsolved philosophical problem.

item.title: Monday Brief: Guyana Lectures the Caribbean on Climate, Then Drowns
item.description: Guyana told the Caribbean to climate-proof their infrastructure, then spent the weekend underwater. Also: Guyoil raised gas prices, the AFC says the 50% profit share is a fantasy, and we got the handrails on the US$6.8B FPSO.

item.title: Speedeet & Wilar: De Day De Rain Come Down
item.description: Georgetown flooding again, and Speedeet see it as an opportunity. Wilar see it as a problem. Dem both right.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh: De Brief Forget To Mention All De GOOD Tings Happening!
item.description: Uncle Ramesh reads de Sunday Brief and has some thoughts about what dem left out. As usual.

item.title: Sunday Brief: Georgetown Flood, Karpowership Extension & The Streets That Used To Be Ours
item.description: Georgetown flooded again, the powerships are staying longer than expected, the government and the Mayor are fighting over who owns the streets, and Rihanna's makeup is now available in Guyana. Sunday, bai.

item.title: 🚗 DJ Roadblock Traffic Report — Friday, March 27, 2026
item.description: DJ Roadblock is LIVE from the dashboard on a Friday afternoon. The government just claimed 22 streets, the potholes are now somebody else's problem, and Camp Street is a philosophical question.

item.title: Guyana Daily Brief — Friday, March 27, 2026
item.description: Government grabs 22 Georgetown streets, Exxon builds an 8th oil ship without approval, and Kristi Noem visits State House. Plus: a rubber duck, a stolen $7M, and Uncle Ramesh has thoughts.

item.title: Patriots Portfolio — March 27, 2026: Streets, Ships, and Sovereignty
item.description: This week: Exxon starts building Oil Ship No. 8 without government approval, the government takes 22 Georgetown streets, and Guyana keeps printing economic records. The Patriots Portfolio tracks the business of Guyana.

item.title: The Guyanese Horizon — March 2026: The City and the Streets
item.description: The Guyanese Horizon — March 2026. Georgetown is changing faster than at any point since independence. New highways, contested streets, a growing skyline. What does the capital look like when a country strikes oil and starts spending?

item.title: Uncle Ramesh — Friday, March 27, 2026
item.description: Uncle Ramesh reads the Chronicle and sees nothing but progress. The streets, the oil, the cash grant — all going according to plan. As usual.

item.title: Back-A-Truck — March 24, 2026: This Week's Deals and Market Finds
item.description: Your weekly guide to what's moving, what's worth buying, and what the market is saying this week in Guyana.

item.title: Bounty Board — March 24, 2026: Community Notices, Events & More
item.description: This week's community notices, events, and things worth knowing about across Guyana.

item.title: DJ Roadblock's Traffic & Infrastructure Report — March 24, 2026
item.description: DJ Roadblock with your weekly road conditions, infrastructure updates, and unsolicited opinions on electric motorcycles.

item.title: DJ Roadblock's Friday Traffic Report: More Road, More Traffic, More Vex
item.description: DJ Roadblock brings you the Friday traffic chaos report — Dem Boys nailed it: more road just means more traffic. Plus the Eccles roundabout is still a parking lot, and the East Bank corridor has drivers questioning their life choices.

item.title: 🚗 DJ Roadblock Traffic Report - Friday, January 30, 2026
item.description: DJ Roadblock with your Friday traffic update - Stanleytown bridge delays, Eccles roundabout chaos, and weekend getaway tips.

item.title: Speedeet & Wilar: De Chinese Restaurant Adventure
item.description: When Wilar’s uncle knows the owner of a Chinese restaurant near Kitty Cinema, the boys get a behind-the-scenes tour that changes everything Speedeet thought he knew about fried rice.

item.title: DJ Roadblock: Weekend Traffic Report & Road Vibes
item.description: DJ Roadblock brings you the Friday evening traffic situation and weekend road conditions across Guyana.

item.title: The Rumor Mill: What Georgetown Whispering This Week
item.description: The completely fictional whispers making rounds in Georgetown this week. All names changed, all situations imagined, all entertainment guaranteed.

item.title: 🚗 DJ Roadblock Traffic Report: January 19, 2026
item.description: Yuh weekly guide to navigating de madness on Guyana roads. West Ruimveldt crying, Regent Street swimming, and de cones multiplying!

item.title: The New Georgetown: How Guyana's Capital is Transforming
item.description:
A Monthly Feature Celebrating Guyana’s Progress

Walk down Main Street today, and you’ll see something remarkable happening. Georgetown, once known for its colonial charm mixed with urban challenges, is undergoing a transformation that would have seemed impossible just five years ago.

The Numbers Tell the Story

Since 2020, Georgetown has seen:

  • 40+ new businesses opening on Main Street alone
  • $500 million invested in waterfront development
  • 300% increase in tourism infrastructure
  • 12 new hotels under construction or recently opened

Beyond the Statistics

But the real story isn’t in the numbers—it’s in the people.

item.title: DJ Roadblock Traffic Report: January 16, 2026
item.description:
🎙️ WAAAAAH GWAAN GEORGETOWN! Is ya boy DJ Roadblock comin’ at you LIVE from de dashboard of me lil Raum, stuck behind a minibus dat ain’t move in 20 minutes!

🚗 This Week’s Road Disasters

Sheriff Street Crater Update: Dat pothole by de KFC? She get BIGGER, family. Man tell me he see a Fielder go down in deh last Tuesday and ain’t come back out yet. If you driving Sheriff Street, say a prayer and hug de left side.
